title: R3.4a: Formulate D4 research questions — K_agent harvest epistemology
description: Formulate D4 research questions. (1) How does spec define boundary between agent knowledge (ephemeral) and store knowledge (durable)? (2) Formal model of knowledge decay vs k* attention capacity? (3) Can we detect agent knowledge not yet in store (harvest gap)? (4) What signals indicate harvest-readiness (token pressure, topic shift, session age)? (5) Relation to BILATERAL spec? Output: question set for D4 subagent.
close_reason: R3.4a: D4 harvest epistemology complete. Report at D4-harvest-epistemology.md. LLM-assisted detection at Stage 0.

title: R3.2a: Formulate D2 research questions — Datalog engine selection criteria
description: Formulate D2 research questions. (1) Which Rust Datalog engines support our subset (conjunctive queries, negation-as-failure, aggregation)? Candidates: datafrog, crepe, ascent, differential-datalog. (2) Minimum Datalog subset for Stage 0 query (INV-QUERY-001-005)? (3) Can semi-naive evaluation (INV-QUERY-004) run on each candidate? (4) Embedding trade-offs (library vs DSL vs hand-rolled)? (5) Interaction with stratum safety (INV-QUERY-003)? Output: structured question set for D2 subagent.
close_reason: R3.2a: D2 Datalog engine survey complete. Report at D2-datalog-engines.md. Custom evaluator recommended (~2100-3400 LOC).

title: R1.4a: Audit all Value enum references across spec/ and guide/
description: The Stage-tag Value enum needs comprehensive documentation with exactly 9 Stage 0 variants and 4 deferred. Audit: (1) Every place spec/ and guide/ reference Value or its variants. (2) Identify any variant names that differ between files. (3) Catalog which variants are used in which namespace. (4) Identify any Stage 1+ variants that leak into Stage 0 code paths. Output: comprehensive reference table.

title: EPIC: R7 — Final Verification & Convergence
description: Final pass: multi-agent convergence verification of entire spec+guide, update all cross-references, propagate naming fixes, verification matrix update, FAILURE_MODES.md incorporation, audit triage finalization. Uses multiple Opus 4.6 subagents to verify final spec/guide for full convergence. All namespaces must achieve research-grade spec maturity, full guide alignment, research-grade type coherence, and Stage 0 readiness. Ref: V1_AUDIT_3-3-2026.md §7.
close_reason: All 4 workstreams complete: R7.1 convergence verified (all namespaces READY, 7/7 checks pass), R7.2 verification matrix updated, R7.3 cross-ref coherence confirmed, R7.4 triage finalized.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: R7 is the definition of done. Every criterion must pass: 0 divergences, 0 phantom types, 100% traceability, 100% verification feasibility, 100% bootstrap compliance. The multi-agent verification (R7.1) runs the same audit methodology as V1 but should find 0 issues. If it finds issues, we iterate until convergence. This is the cleanroom quality gate.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: R7 is the convergence gate — nothing ships until R7 passes. Four verification tasks: (1) R7.1 multi-agent final verification (per-namespace readiness + automated scan + 100% targets). (2) R7.2 verification matrix update for all new/changed INVs. (3) R7.3 cross-reference coherence (zero orphans, zero dangling refs). (4) R7.4 triage resolution summary (close out V1_AUDIT_TRIAGE.md, move to audits/resolved/). R7.1c is the capstone: it verifies ALL five 100% targets (CRDT soundness, verification, traceability, bootstrap, type coherence). Only after R7.4 confirms everything is resolved do we declare audit complete and move to Stage 0 implementation.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: CONTAINER: Do not claim this epic directly. This is the final convergence gate. Work R7.1a, R7.1b, R7.2a when they unblock. Close this epic when all children are done.

title: R7.1: Multi-agent final convergence verification (V2 audit)
description:
Launch multiple Opus 4.6 subagents to verify the final spec+guide achieves full convergence after all R0-R6 remediation.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA (ALL must pass):
1. ALL namespaces: research-grade spec maturity
2. ALL namespaces: full guide alignment (0 divergences)
3. ALL namespaces: research-grade type coherence (0 phantom, 0 divergent)
4. ALL namespaces: Stage 0 Ready
5. 100% CRDT algebraic correctness (proven + verified)
6. 100% self-bootstrap compliance
7. 100% verification pipeline feasibility
8. 100% bilateral traceability
9. 0 spec-internal contradictions
10. 0 spec-guide divergences

APPROACH: 7 subagents (one per namespace pair) + 3 cross-cutting subagents = 10 total verification agents. Each must confirm 0 remaining issues in their scope.

DEPENDS ON: ALL R0-R6 tasks complete.

DELIVERABLE: V2_AUDIT report confirming full convergence, or listing remaining issues for remediation.

SOURCES: All 14 audit agents (baseline), user directive.
TRACES: V1_AUDIT_3-3-2026.md, SEED.md.
close_reason: R7.1a: all namespaces READY. R7.1b: 7/7 checks pass. R7.1c: all 100% targets confirmed.

title: R7.1a: Per-namespace Stage 0 readiness verification (10 namespaces)
description:
Final verification that ALL 10 namespaces achieve Stage 0 readiness.

PER-NAMESPACE CHECKLIST (must ALL pass):

□ STORE: Value enum stage-tagged, EntityId defined, Store API consistent, serialization specified
□ SCHEMA: Schema struct contradiction resolved, API surface unified, SchemaError defined
□ QUERY: QueryExpr reconciled, Clause variants unified, QueryResult type agreed, Datalog guidance added
□ RESOLUTION: LWW tie-breaking settled, ResolutionMode reconciled, Conflict type unified, causal independence fixed
□ HARVEST: HarvestCandidate reconciled, drift_score type settled, API as free functions, K_agent reframed
□ SEED: SeedOutput canonical, section names unified, budget compliance specified, CLAUDE.md ownership settled
□ MERGE: INV-MERGE-008 renumbered, MergeReceipt reconciled, CRDT proofs complete, cascade specified
□ GUIDANCE: GuidanceFooter enriched, DriftSignals defined, methodology pipeline typed, footer selection specified
□ INTERFACE: MCPServer model settled, MCP_TOOLS typed, ToolResponse in spec, error protocol complete
□ BUDGET: All Stage 1 INVs properly tagged, token counting strategy defined, no Stage 0 leaks

ALSO VERIFY:
- types.md entry exists for every Stage 0 type
- Every Stage 0 INV has a guide three-box decomposition
- Every Stage 0 INV has a verification strategy (V: tag + harness)
- Every Stage 0 ADR has rationale and alternatives

QUALITY CRITERION: 10/10 namespaces pass. Zero blockers remain.

title: R4.1a: Classify 21 phantom types by stage and provenance
description: Take the 21 phantom types identified by the audit (types referenced in spec/guide but never defined anywhere). For each: (1) Determine if it belongs to Stage 0 or Stage 1+. (2) Identify which file references it and in what context. (3) Classify as: DEFINE (needs formal type definition), ALIAS (is actually a renamed version of an existing type), REMOVE (truly orphaned reference that should be deleted). This is triage — no changes to files yet. Output: classification table with stage, provenance, and recommended action per type.

(2) Within each namespace, rank by severity. (3) Identify which divergences are already covered by other beads (e.g., type divergences covered by R1, contradictions covered by R0). (4) Identify net-new divergences requiring standalone fixes. Output: ranked resolution list with cross-references to existing beads.
